design_intent: Selective merge for Linkding hardv3 Focus20: keep V2.5 hidden-click and checkpoint recovery while restoring V2.4 query finalization plus login next/prefilled form protections.

evidence_scope.primary_slice: Focus20 hardv3 matched V2.4 vs V2.5 trajectories
evidence_scope.held_out_policy: TaskBank36 is reserved for validation after Focus20 gates; it is not used to write these rules.

rules.title: After one hidden or timed-out click, discard the same bid and re-observe

trigger.old_assumption: A target that appears in the DOM can be clicked again after a visibility or timeout failure.
observed_symptoms: The last action failed because the element was hidden, not visible, not stable, timed-out, or could not be found.
The URL and visible state did not move toward the goal after the failed click.

adaptation_strategy: Never click the same bid again after one hidden or timed-out click.
Discard the stale bid, re-observe, and choose a different visible link, button, route fallback, visible continuation, or final answer.
Prefer visible text, aria labels, href semantics, and current-page evidence over a remembered numeric bid.
verification_check: The next action after the failure uses a different visible target, fallback route, or send_msg_to_user.
Recovery should change menu visibility, URL, form visibility, or final evidence.
forbidden_actions: Do not repeat the same bid after an invisible-element timeout.
Do not treat DOM presence of a hidden target as human-clickable evidence.

rules.title: Use one same-site route fallback, then reassess before any second route

trigger.old_assumption: The agent must keep searching for a drifted navigation affordance even when the requested same-site route is obvious.
observed_symptoms: The task asks only to land on settings, tags, bookmarks, or the add-bookmark form.
A visible navigation attempt failed once, or the page remains on the wrong same-site route.

adaptation_strategy: After one failed navigation attempt, use exactly one direct local route fallback on the current origin, such as /settings, /tags, /bookmarks, or /bookmarks/new.
After the route loads, do not immediately issue another goto; reassess URL, headings, visible fields, staged checkpoint controls, and final evidence.
Finalize if the route and visible state satisfy the task; otherwise click one visible continuation control and reassess.
verification_check: The URL path matches the requested destination before additional browser actions.
The next step after route fallback is finalization, one visible continuation, or one different visible recovery target.
forbidden_actions: Do not chain multiple same-site gotos without observing the new page.
Do not use route fallback for tasks that still require saving or editing data.

rules.title: Click staged continuation only when final evidence is absent

trigger.old_assumption: Destination route arrival is always enough, or repeated noop is safer than one checkpoint action.
observed_symptoms: The route is plausible but final evidence such as fields, committed query, or requested heading is absent.
The page shows a visible continuation control such as Review, Apply, Open, Continue, Save options, or a subpanel opener.

adaptation_strategy: First check whether final evidence is already present; if it is, send the final answer immediately.
Only when final evidence is absent, click one visible continuation control that semantically advances the staged page.
After that one continuation, reassess URL, headings, visible fields, exact query state, and filtered result evidence before taking another action.
verification_check: A continuation click should reveal fields, committed filter state, a destination panel, or other final evidence.
A satisfied state should finalize instead of entering noop or goto loops.
forbidden_actions: Do not click Apply, Open, or Continue after final evidence is already visible.
Do not noop repeatedly on a staged checkpoint page.

rules.title: Preserve task-specific login next redirects and prefilled form values

trigger.old_assumption: After a login redirect, replaying a generic login or generic add-bookmark example is a safe recovery.
observed_symptoms: The task starts on /login?next=... with encoded destination, URL, title, tag, or query values.
After login, the browser leaves /login and lands on a protected destination or prefilled form.

adaptation_strategy: Fill username and password once, submit once, then operate on the redirected page instead of returning to /login.
Preserve the task-specific next parameter and any redirected prefilled URL, title, tag, or query values.
If the redirected page or prefilled form already proves the requested state, send_msg_to_user with the required completion words.
verification_check: The current route should stay on the redirected destination after login.
The visible form, URL, or query state should retain task-specific prefilled values instead of generic examples.
forbidden_actions: Do not navigate back to /login after a successful redirect.
Do not strip the next parameter.
Do not replace prefilled values with generic example data.
Do not save a bookmark unless the task explicitly asks to persist it.

rules.title: Use a visible opener for collapsed navigation, then retry once or fall back

trigger.old_assumption: A hidden destination can be clicked directly because it appears in pruned HTML.
observed_symptoms: A destination like Settings, Tags, Bookmarks, or a subpanel is present but the click target is not visible.
Navigation groups are nested, collapsed, or reorganized behind a visible group label.

adaptation_strategy: Click one visible opener or group label first, then re-observe and retry the now-visible destination once.
If the destination remains hidden, switch to one direct local route fallback instead of repeating the hidden target.
Use visible text or href semantics from the new observation, not the previous hidden bid.
verification_check: The opener should reveal the destination, or the fallback should change the URL to the requested same-site path.
The trace should not contain a long same-bid loop on a hidden nav item.
forbidden_actions: Do not repeatedly click a hidden destination inside a collapsed group.
Do not click the same hidden bid after an opener fails to reveal it.

rules.title: Avoid non-interactive marks and containers when a visible semantic control exists

trigger.old_assumption: Any bid-like mark suggested by the model is likely safe to click.
observed_symptoms: The candidate target is a hidden input, CSRF token, divider, list item, shell, or generic container.
A nearby visible link, button, textbox, or explicit continuation control has clearer task semantics.

adaptation_strategy: Choose the visible semantic control rather than a hidden input, CSRF token, divider, or non-interactive container.
If no valid visible target exists and the task is navigation-only, use one same-site route fallback.
If the page already satisfies the task, finalize instead of clicking a questionable mark.
verification_check: Chosen targets should be visible human-clickable links, buttons, textboxes, or explicit continuation controls.
The next state should either progress toward the goal or expose final evidence.
forbidden_actions: Do not click hidden inputs, CSRF tokens, dividers, or generic containers.
Do not treat structural DOM presence as proof of clickability.

rules.title: For filtered bookmarks, finalize exact query evidence before apply or commit recovery

trigger.old_assumption: A filtered bookmarks task always needs another apply or commit action after arriving on bookmarks.
observed_symptoms: The URL already contains the requested q parameter, or a visible search/filter field contains the exact query.
The page may also show a prepared lookup, chip, Apply button, or other commit control.

adaptation_strategy: If exact query evidence is already present in the URL or visible field, treat the filtered result as complete and send_msg_to_user immediately.
If exact query evidence is absent but a prepared lookup or query chip is visible, click one Apply or commit control, then verify the exact query.
Do not clear, rewrite, or replace a task-specific query with a generic search.
verification_check: Final evidence should include the exact requested query in the URL, visible field, chip, or filtered result state.
The final answer should state that the filtered bookmarks page is open.
forbidden_actions: Do not click Apply or commit when exact query evidence is already active.
Do not wait for invisible confirmation after the filtered result is already visible.
Do not clear a task-specific q parameter.

rules.title: Hard stop repeated noop, goto, or click loops after one recovery attempt

trigger.old_assumption: Repeating noop, the same goto, or the same plausible click is safer than switching strategy.
observed_symptoms: Two consecutive actions leave URL and visible page state unchanged.
The trace repeats noop, the same goto, or the same click while a plausible final state or checkpoint is already visible.

adaptation_strategy: Use a hard stop after one failed recovery attempt: do not repeat noop, the same goto, or the same click.
Switch to exactly one of these: finalize from satisfied evidence, click one visible continuation, use one route fallback, or choose one different visible semantic target.
Keep enough step budget for verification instead of spending it on identical retries.
verification_check: A stuck trace should change strategy within two steps.
The final action should be a verified route, a valid visible target, or send_msg_to_user.
forbidden_actions: Do not burn the remaining budget on identical clicks, gotos, waits, or noops.
Do not continue browser actions after benchmark completion is evident.
